name: Oldsmar Chemical Overdose
real_world_reference: Oldsmar, Florida water treatment plant attack — February 5, 2021
description: An attacker used TeamViewer to remotely access the Oldsmar water treatment plant SCADA workstation and raised the NaOH (sodium hydroxide) setpoint from the safe level of 111 ppm to 11,100 ppm — a 100x increase that would render the water caustic. A vigilant operator noticed the cursor moving on screen. The agent must detect the intrusion, sever the remote session, restore the safe setpoint, acknowledge the root-cause alarm, and document the incident.
attack_vector: Remote desktop (TeamViewer) exploitation — attacker gained access via unpatched TeamViewer on an internet-exposed SCADA workstation
hints: The remote session IP 185.220.x.x is a Tor exit node — this is not a legitimate vendor.
NaOH_SETPOINT_PPM in the historian will reveal the attacker's target value.
Isolating the network segment will terminate the attacker's ability to hold the setpoint.
ALM-NaOH-01 is first_out=True — it is the root cause alarm, not a nuisance alarm.
